title: Production-unsafe Django secrets and debug mode are hard-coded
reasoning: The settings module hard-codes the Django SECRET_KEY and PostgreSQL credentials while also enabling DEBUG and leaving ALLOWED_HOSTS empty. If this settings module is deployed or committed/shared as-is, disclosure of the secret key can allow session/signing forgery risks, database credentials are exposed, and DEBUG can leak sensitive runtime details. The ASGI/WSGI entrypoints always load this same settings module, so there is no separate production-safe configuration path in the owned files.
recommendation: Read SECRET_KEY, DEBUG, ALLOWED_HOSTS, and database credentials from environment variables or a deployment secret store, default DEBUG to false for non-local environments, and fail startup when required production settings are missing.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included for settings validation or production deployment safety.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a settings smoke test that imports the production settings environment with DEBUG disabled and asserts secrets/DB credentials are not literal defaults and required host configuration is present.
minimumFixScope: Update MySite/settings.py to externalize secrets and environment-specific deployment settings.
